aliases: Pasta with Tomato Sauce and Mushrooms

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pasta food product class (FOODON_00002473); no specific tomato-mushroom pasta entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers cooked pasta as a category. No specific tomato-mushroom pasta composite code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229961001 covers pasta as a food concept; no specific tomato-mushroom pasta variant exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of pasta with tomato sauce and mushrooms is approximately 350 g, comprising cooked pasta, tomato-based sauce, and sautéed mushrooms.

nutrient_source.title: Pasta with tomato sauce (cooked) — composite estimate based on USDA FoodData Central entries: Pasta, cooked, enriched (FDC ID 20420) and Tomato sauce, canned (FDC ID 11549) and Mushrooms, cooked (FDC ID 11260)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for pasta with tomato sauce and mushrooms as a composite dish. Values estimated from weighted combination: approximately 60% cooked pasta (FDC 20420), 30% tomato sauce (FDC 11549), 10% cooked mushrooms (FDC 11260), reflecting a typical restaurant-style preparation. Energy and macros cross-checked against NCC Food and Nutrient Database category anchors for pasta dishes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; pasta, tomato sauce, cooked
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 45 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for cooked pasta with tomato sauce (spaghetti with tomato sauce, GI approximately 40–50, mean ~45). Mushrooms contribute negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 71.75) / 100 ≈ 32.3, rounded to 32.5. Serving carbohydrate = 20.5 g per 100 g × 350 g = 71.75 g.

hypertension.guidance: A 350 g restaurant serving provides approximately 735 mg sodium, representing about 32–49% of the recommended daily limit of 1500–2300 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-salt sauce or limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from refined pasta carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 45 and a glycemic load of ~32 per standard serving, this dish has a moderate glycemic impact; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a smaller portion (150–200 g), choose wholegrain pasta where available, and pair with a protein source to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and absence of cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is low in saturated fat (approximately 1.05 g per serving) and contains no cholesterol, making it a heart-friendly option; however, sodium from tomato sauce should be monitored, and olive oil quantity should be kept moderate to maintain a favourable fat profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pasta with Tomato Sauce and Mushrooms is a classic Italian-inspired pasta dish consisting of cooked pasta (typically spaghetti or penne) tossed in a freshly prepared tomato-based sauce and sautéed mushrooms, often finished with olive oil, garlic, and fresh or dried herbs such as basil and oregano. The dish is widely served across Western and global-fusion restaurants in Singapore, including at casual dining establishments. The texture combines the soft chew of al-dente pasta with the umami depth of mushrooms and the bright acidity of tomato sauce. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-energy dish (~112 kcal per 100 g) with a carbohydrate-dominant macronutrient profile (~20.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g), low saturated fat (~0.3 g per 100 g), and no cholesterol, making it suitable for cardiovascular health. The glycemic index is approximately 45 (low GI), though the glycemic load per standard restaurant serving (~350 g) is moderate at approximately 32–33, which is relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is moderate (~210 mg per 100 g) and primarily derived from the tomato sauce, which is a consideration for individuals with hypertension. The dish is naturally vegetarian, vegan, and halal-friendly, and contains gluten from wheat-based pasta. It is a good source of complex carbohydrates, dietary fibre, and B vitamins, and provides modest amounts of iron, potassium, and vitamin C from the tomato sauce component.

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source entry exists for this exact composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted combination of USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked pasta, tomato sauce, and cooked mushrooms. GI is based on an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for pasta with tomato sauce. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central (cooked pasta FDC 20420, tomato sauce FDC 11549, cooked mushrooms FDC 11260) using an approximate 60:30:10 weight ratio. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero for this plant-based pasta dish with no animal fats; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component sources. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this dish contains no animal-derived ingredients in its base formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based dish with no animal products; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component sources. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.8), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.3), potassium_mg (185.0), calcium_mg (14.0), iron_mg (0.9), vitamin_c_mg (5.2), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (18.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.08), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.06), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (1.1), vitamin_b6_mg (0.09), folate_mcg_dfe (22.0), vitamin_d_mcg (0.1), vitamin_e_mg (0.6), phosphorus_mg (68.0), magnesium_mg (18.0), zinc_mg (0.5), selenium_mcg (12.0), water_g (72.0) are all estimated from USDA FoodData Central component sources. 6. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for spaghetti with tomato sauce; glycemic load per 350 g serving = (45 × 71.75) / 100 ≈ 32.5. 7. The input food_name 'Pasta Tomato Shrooms' from field survey data (Flavours by Saute - Funan) has been normalised to the canonical English name 'Pasta with Tomato Sauce and Mushrooms'. 8. This dish is served at SGD 15.00 at Flavours by Saute - Funan on field survey data. 9. Cuisine is classified as Italian / Western given the dish composition; the food was encountered in a Singapore delivery context. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) given the Singapore serving context. 10. All -1 sentinel values have been replaced with estimated numeric values from USDA component sources; no -1 values remain in per_100g.
